title: foundations
purpose: Defines the visual and semantic foundation of a design system: tokens, scales, primitives, and naming.
when: When creating or refactoring design token systems, color palettes, typography scales, spacing, radii, elevation, motion primitives, or breakpoints.

title: components
purpose: Designs reusable component contracts, states, variants, and composition rules for a scalable UI library.
when: When designing or reviewing component APIs, variants, states, slots, composition rules, or interaction behavior.

title: theming
purpose: Defines multi-theme, light/dark, and multi-brand theming with semantic safety and implementation discipline.
when: When adding light/dark mode, brand themes, high-contrast support, or semantic color contracts to a design system.

title: accessibility
purpose: Bakes accessibility into tokens, components, theming, and documentation at the system level.
when: When ensuring design system tokens meet contrast requirements, components have proper focus/keyboard/screen-reader support, or motion respects reduced-motion preferences.

title: documentation
purpose: Documents system intent, usage rules, component contracts, and adoption guidance for design and engineering teams.
when: When writing or reviewing design system documentation, component docs, migration guides, or adoption materials.

title: governance
purpose: Defines contribution, review, versioning, deprecation, and ownership rules for sustainable design systems.
when: When establishing contribution workflows, review gates, versioning policy, deprecation paths, or ownership models for a design system.

title: review-audit
purpose: Audits products or libraries against design-system rules and produces concrete remediation guidance.
when: When auditing interfaces, component libraries, or codebases for design-system compliance, token misuse, or consistency issues.

title: framework-mapping
purpose: Maps design-system concepts to CSS, Tailwind, CSS Modules, utility-first systems, and CSS-in-JS without losing semantic intent.
when: When translating design-system tokens and components into CSS, SCSS, Tailwind, CSS Modules, or CSS-in-JS implementations.
